'''Fletcher Rooney''' (born 12 January 2006) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|fb}} for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Rooney was born in [[West Yorkshire]], England. He is the son of former England international rugby league footballer [[Jamie Rooney]].<ref name="LRL NextGen">{{Cite web |last=Heppenstall |first=Ross |date=30 July 2024 |title=NextGen: Meet the son of a Super League icon tipped to become a Castleford Tigers star |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/nextgen-meet-the-son-of-a-super-league-icon-tipped-to-become-a-castleford-tigers-star |access-date=5 August 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

Rooney played junior rugby league for [[Featherstone Lions]] and [[Castleford Lock Lane|Lock Lane ARLFC]]. He joined the [[Castleford Tigers]] development system at scholarship level.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Harber |first=Tony |date=18 December 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers delighted that promising young full-back Fletcher Rooney has committed to the club |url=https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-delighted-that-promising-young-full-back-fletcher-rooney-has-committed-to-the-club-4449596 |access-date=22 January 2024 |website=Wakefield Express}}</ref> He overcame an [[ACL injury]] at the age of 14.<ref name="LRL NextGen" />

In September 2022, Rooney progressed to Castleford's academy squad.<ref>{{Cite web |date=1 September 2022 |title=Tigers stepping up our youth ranks |url=https://castlefordtigersfoundation.co.uk/news/tigers-stepping-up-our-youth-ranks/ |access-date=8 November 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers Foundation}}</ref> During the 2023 academy season, he made 11 appearances and scored 10 tries and 1 goal, as well as a further 5 appearances and 1 try at Reserves level, and was named Academy Player of the Year.<ref>{{Cite web |date=17 September 2023 |title=Tigers host end of season dinner |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8761 |access-date=8 November 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref>

Rooney represented [[Yorkshire rugby league team|Yorkshire]] in their Academy Origin fixture against [[Lancashire rugby league team|Lancashire]] on 19 August 2023.<ref>{{Cite web |date=10 August 2023 |title=Academy Origin III: Squads named for series decider |url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/62029/academy-origin-iii-squads-named-for-series-decider--- |access-date=8 November 2023 |website=The RFL}}</ref> He played as a {{Rlp|ce}} in the 44–16 defeat at [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]'s [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=20 August 2023 |title=Academy Origin III: Lancashire 44 Yorkshire 16 |url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/62060/academy-origin-iii-lancashire-44-yorkshire-16--- |access-date=8 November 2023 |website=The RFL}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=22 August 2023 |title=Rooney reflects on Yorkshire experience |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8732 |access-date=8 November 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref>

==Playing career==
In September 2023, Rooney was introduced into first-team training with the [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] senior squad, and was assigned squad number 47.<ref name="CT Squad for Wigan">{{Cite web |date=13 September 2023 |title=Tigers select squad for Wigan |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8756 |access-date=8 November 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> On 22 September, aged 17, he made his [[Super League]] debut for Castleford Tigers against [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the final round of the [[2023 Castleford Tigers season|season]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=22 September 2023 |title=Super League: Leeds Rhinos 46-0 Castleford Tigers - Hosts put big losses behind them with win |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66870796 |access-date=8 November 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=22 September 2023 |title='Emphatic' Leeds Rhinos on right end of a rout as Castleford Tigers collapse |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/emphatic-leeds-rhinos-on-right-end-of-a-rout-as-castleford-tigers-collapse-4346521 |access-date=8 November 2023 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref> His performance in defeat earned praise from head coaches [[Danny Ward (rugby league)|Danny Ward]] and [[Rohan Smith (rugby league)|Rohan Smith]],<ref name="SARL Breakout stars">{{Cite web |last=Cook |first=Sam |date=30 November 2023 |title=Predicting five breakout stars of the 2024 Super League season |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/predicting-five-breakout-stars-of-the-2024-super-league-season-85572/ |access-date=13 December 2023 |website=Serious About Rugby League}}</ref> as well as the travelling fans.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Cook |first=Sam |date=23 September 2023 |title="Joke of a club" – Furious Castleford Tigers fans call for change after Leeds Rhinos defeat |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/is-danny-ward-really-the-answer-castleford-tigers-fans-left-questioning-the-future-after-humiliating-defeat-82452/ |access-date=8 November 2023 |website=Serious About Rugby League}}</ref> Rooney said of his debut, "All my life I've been working towards that moment, it was a big thing for me and my family."<ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=12 December 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers tie down young gun to long-term contract as full-back lays out 2024 ambitions |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-tie-down-young-gun-fletcher-rooney-to-long-term-contract-as-full-back-lays-out-2024-ambitions |access-date=13 December 2023 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> He became the first 2006-born player to feature in the Super League.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Ibbetson |first=Stephen |date=18 December 2023 |title=Fletcher Rooney joins Castleford Tigers squad keen to turn focus on youth |url=https://www.totalrl.com/fletcher-rooney-joins-castleford-tigers-squad-keen-to-turn-focus-on-youth/ |access-date=18 December 2023 |website=TotalRL}}</ref>

Ahead of the [[2024 Castleford Tigers season|2024 season]], Rooney was included in Castleford's listed first-team squad in October 2023,<ref>{{Cite web |date=22 October 2023 |title=Full 2024 first team squad |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8796 |access-date=8 November 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers |archive-date=6 November 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231106200050/https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8796 |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=23 October 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers reveal 30-man squad for Super League 2024 |url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-reveal-30-man-squad-for-super-league-2024/ |access-date=8 November 2023 |website=TotalRL}}</ref> one of only two fullbacks alongside [[Luke Hooley]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=11 January 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers fans' fears eased after rising star suffers training injury |url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-fans-fears-eased-after-rising-star-suffers-training-injury/ |access-date=22 January 2024 |website=TotalRL}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=18 January 2024 |title=Super League: A young gun from every club to keep an eye out for in 2024 |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/super-league-a-young-gun-from-every-club-to-keep-an-eye-out-for-in-2024 |access-date=22 January 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> He would be following a hybrid programme between the academy and first-team environments to support his development.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=12 December 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers hand long-term deal to highly-rated youngster ahead of 2024 Super League season |url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-hand-long-term-deal-to-highly-rated-youngster-ahead-of-2024-super-league-season/ |access-date=13 December 2023 |website=TotalRL}}</ref> In December 2023, it was announced that Rooney had signed his first professional contract with Castleford, agreeing a three-year deal.<ref>{{Cite web |date=12 December 2023 |title=Rooney signs until 2026 |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8833 |access-date=13 December 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Matthew |date=12 December 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers tie down rising talent to first professional contract |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-tie-down-rising-28278167 |access-date=13 December 2023 |website=Yorkshire Live}}</ref> He required surgery for an ankle injury suffered in January,<ref>{{Cite web |date=15 March 2024 |title=Medical update |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8912 |access-date=6 August 2024 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> which prevented him filling in for the injured Hooley before the Tigers signed [[Tex Hoy]].<ref name="LRL NextGen" /> On 1 August, playing as a {{Rlp|wg}}er, Rooney made his second senior appearance and scored his first Super League try in Castleford's home loss to [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=1 August 2024 |title=Leigh boost play-off hopes as they beat Castleford |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c880zvjl3nko |access-date=5 August 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Harber |first=Tony |date=6 August 2024 |title=Hometown boys like Fletcher Rooney are key to Castleford Tigers' plans going forward |url=https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hometown-boys-like-fletcher-rooney-are-key-to-castleford-tigers-plans-going-forward-4730230 |access-date=6 August 2024 |website=Wakefield Express}}</ref> He scored a second try on 13 September against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in his third appearance.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Harby |first=Chris |date=13 September 2024 |title=Saints close in on play-off spot with Cas victory |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c3rdrrp8rx3o |access-date=24 May 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

Rooney began the [[2025 Castleford Tigers season|2025 season]] starting at fullback for Castleford, owing to Hoy covering halfback for the injured [[Rowan Milnes]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Chapman Coombe |first=Louis |date=14 February 2025 |title=Danny McGuire details potential Castleford Tigers spine combination for Super League opener |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/danny-mcguire-details-potential-castleford-tigers-spine-combination-for-super-league-opener |access-date=23 May 2025 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> His strong performances, particularly against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in round 1, drew praise from head coach [[Danny McGuire]] who said, "He will be a superstar once we've finished with him."<ref>{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Matthew |date=15 February 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers unearth superstar with teenager tipped to reach the top |url=https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/ca-976795 |access-date=23 May 2025 |website=All Out Rugby League}}</ref> He subsequently signed a new five-year contract with Castleford in February, and said, "I've worked hard at every level to be where I am today and I'm ambitious about the future."<ref>{{Cite web |date=18 February 2025 |title=Castleford full-back Rooney signs five-year deal |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c8rkxjdr52ko |access-date=23 May 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Cook |first=Sam |date=18 February 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers sign talent to five-year deal in huge statement of intent |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/castleford-tigers-sign-talent-to-five-year-deal-in-huge-statement-of-intent-101682/ |access-date=23 May 2025 |website=Serious About Rugby League}}</ref> In March, Rooney's impressive start to the season was cut short by a high-grade quadriceps injury suffered in training, expected to rule him out for around four months.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Tomlinson |first=Daniel |date=17 March 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers confirm serious injury blow with full prognosis revealed |url=https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/castleford-tigers-confirm-serious-injury-1035969 |access-date=23 May 2025 |website=All Out Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=17 March 2025 |title=Tigers full-back Rooney sidelined for four months |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cr52pnr8jd9o |access-date=23 May 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>
